author: @ronniepirtlejr2606
text:
I like listening to music that I pick out to luke.
 On the radio they constantly bombard you with commercials.
I feel like they are trying to brainwash you into remembering their products. Lol well, ...to think about it, .. they are!

author: @ronniepirtlejr2606
text:
I would recommend the first thing to do is before plugging ANY Electronics stereo in the future Luke i(f you value it) 
Is connecting it to a current limiting device first. For instance a lightbulb of comparable wattage in line on your hot side of the A/C line. I'm sure your father has one around there.
 I love your videos!😆👍🇺🇸

author: @richardsandwell2285
text:
Fantastic work there, apparently you can buy units to go into retro type sound equipment which can capture blue tooth signals, you can then play music from your phone into the speakers of the unit. 
The unit you have looks modular so maybe the CD unit could be changed into your Bluetooth receiver and or a USB input stage.

author: @Gwydion67
text:
This just gives me the kick to refurbish my old Hitachi receiver. Didn't use it for years course of the miserable sound quality. But maybe the speakers need a resoldering, or / and the pots need a cleaning. Thanks for the inspiration,  Luke! 😃👍
(BTW: You're lucky that the cassette belt was still on tension.)

author: @GKH
text: Hi Luke. If you have a cdrom from an old PC then you can mount it and use it to play audio cd. Just connect power 5v/12v and audio line.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Awesome! I was wondering what I could do to fix this problem! I couldn't find a replacement part online. so I will give this ago, Thanks mate! :)

author: @NorthernKitty
text: I recently ran across a cassette tape from the 80's that I'd like to listen to, but realize I no longer have any players.  I'll have to be watching for one "in the bin", or perhaps at garage sales! 😋

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I got given a load of cassette tapes ages ago, I'm just glad I don't have to listen to them in the car all the time anymore lol. I hope you're able to find one or pick one up for super cheap, i love this retro equipment! Thank you! :)

author: @endadalton
text: Hi I am currently trying to get my little Sony high working, well the cassette section. I have replaced the belt as it was stretched. But the sound is still rotten it sounds stretched and I have tried to manually adjust the speed but I can’t get a good sound. Help please.

author: @robertpitt8418
text: You said you didn't want to play the music for obvious reasons: i bet it was a clip of yourself singing, if that's the case you made the right decision why take the chance on loosing all your subscribers  : )

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: hahaha, you got me there! I think I made the right decision lol! Thanks mate! :)

author: @ALayne08
text: I love the excitement in Luke’s tone as he talked about and fixed the component. Take care!

author: @NEVUoficial
text: it would be really cool to learn to service cassette decks

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Now that's something I have never done, I will have a look into that. Cheers for the suggestion mate! :)

author: @ianbigland4618
text: I wish you could have shown us what the faults were and how you fixed them

author: @randomlettersqzkebkw
text: is this guy related to robert? he sorta looks like him

author: @stevecummins324
text:
record button not latching?  blank cassettes have tabs on edge away from exposed tape that can be knocked out to prevent recording on tape.. without those tabs, or sticky tape lol, being in place  record usually fails to latch.
also was not uncommon to have to press both record and play at same time for record to latch. 

glad you got it working.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I heard about the sticky tape trick to record over used cassette tapes, it seems this recorder needs to have a tape init before you able to record. Thanks mate! :)

author: @roberthopgood1894
text: Who listens to cassettes now??

replies.author: @Gwydion67
replies.text: Such young man as Luke listening to the retro, analog sound of a cassette player, even recording... That's what I call coool! 📻🎙🎶😎👍

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I do now, It is super cool to have my own custom mixes in the car, instead of listening to the radio. it can get boring. :)

replies.author: @roberthopgood1894
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey You could replace the cd play with an cd burner

author: @johnwinters1705
text: Loved this video... Would you be able to do one on a vhs player?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you mate! :) If I find a broken VHS player I would probably do that.

author: @1JasonBradly
text: Standerd operation not to be able to depress the record button without a cassette with a record tab intact or sticky taped over to engage the record tab sensor.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thanks mate! I thought it was broken at first, after recording my first mixtape everything works really well.! :)

author: @karlmyers6518
text: Nice one Luke , I have lots of memories recorded the chart show on a Sunday as a kid.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thats so cool! I just finished recording my first mixtape using the vinyls I have collected. Cheers mate! :)

author: @azlandpilotcar4450
text: Good video and great project.  An amateur's recommendation: If you're going to resolder, replace the wires at the same time -- they get old quickly!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Awesome suggestion, it didn't occur to me to do that. That is something i will do in the future though. Thanks mate! :)

author: @charleshayden8079
text: Good luck finding anyone to buy it. Enjoying time spent repairing old equipment like this has been good enough for me!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Im just super glad that it works now it has a pride of place with my other music equipment. I really do enjoy repairing this sort of stuff, otherwise, i would never know how this stuff worked. Thanks mate! :)

replies.author: @charleshayden8079
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey  I started my career tearing everything up, at about the age of 5.  Eventually over the years I became a master mechanic, and electronics engineer capable of repairing almost anything.  These days it's difficult for me to justify the time spent repairing   antiquated equipment like this, but I enjoy it and still learn something new about the world every time I do it!

author: @Arachnoid_of_the_underverse
text: Damn there was I waiting you to break out a bit of the Shadows Apache on the guitar lol.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahaha, awesome! I don't know why I didn't play anything now lol. Cheers mate! :)
